short_definition: A phantom black dog of East Anglian folklore.
full_definition: Black Shuck is a spectral black dog in English folklore, particularly in East Anglia. It has been treated as an omen, death-portent, and regional ghost-lore figure, later feeding into broader paranormal and cryptid vocabularies.
location_notes: Especially associated with Norfolk and Suffolk traditions.
cultural_context: English ghost lore, omen traditions, and death-portent narratives.
historical_period: Early modern to present in folklore retention
modern_usage: Still cited in black-dog lore and paranormal tourism.
confidence_level: Low as literal claim; high as folklore

short_definition: The British research society central to formal psychical investigation since 1882.
full_definition: The Society for Psychical Research, founded in London in 1882, became one of the most important institutions in the history of organized psychical investigation. Its work helped shape the vocabulary of mediumship research, apparitions, telepathy, survival inquiry, and controlled scrutiny of paranormal claims.
cultural_context: Victorian and modern psychical research, institutionalized inquiry, and survival studies.
modern_usage: Still invoked as a prestige reference in serious paranormal and survival-research circles.

short_definition: The religious-philosophical system associated with Aleister Crowley.
full_definition: Thelema is the esoteric system articulated by Aleister Crowley, centered on True Will, ritual practice, magical discipline, and a distinctive cosmology. It shaped later occult vocabulary in both the UK and the United States.
cultural_context: Modern occultism, magical orders, and post-Golden Dawn ritual culture.
modern_usage: Foundational term in modern ceremonial and esoteric circles.
source_type: primary occult texts and secondary scholarship
